body.title: test_done_prefix_closes_ticket red — DS close now appends --shipped-unproven args
body.description:
PRE-EXISTING failure (unrelated to the router cutover; found during T-inference-migrate-consumers-cutover). tests/inference/test_dicksimnel.py::TestPostResultGuards::test_done_prefix_closes_ticket asserts _run_queue_cmd(close, T-x, msg) but the DickSimnel close path now also passes --shipped-unproven plus a branch-builder reason. Introduced by commit 1ac42077 (scaffold(builder-merge-proof): shared close mixin + merge-proof stub, T-builder-merge-time-proof) which is CLOSED but left this test red. Decide: update the test to expect the shipped-unproven close args (if the mixin behavior is intended) or fix the close path.

**Test plan:** tests/inference/test_dicksimnel.py::TestPostResultGuards::test_done_prefix_closes_ticket passes.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Test was stale, implementation was right. DS closes shipped-unproven with a named lever because it is a branch-builder: the proof-on-close gate re-runs the test against HEAD, and DS's impl lives on an unmerged branch, so it structurally cannot emit a HEAD-valid proof. Verified the lever is not vapor — devlab/claudecode/builder_merge_proof.emit_merge_proof exists (operator-invoked, not auto-wired at merge; that gap is T-consequence-checking-gap territory, not this ticket). Re-pinned the test on the INTENT (close carries --shipped-unproven and a lever naming branch+proof) and removed a hollow assertion that read the expected close-note out of mock_cmd.call_args, so it could never have failed on a wrong note. Commit 9fdfad27; tests/inference/test_dicksimnel.py 33 passed.
body.unproven_reason: Test-only change: there is no implementation delta between red and green, and proof_emitter requires one (it reverts parent..HEAD minus the test file, which here is empty). No red form exists that a hollow version of this change would fail — a test rewritten to match whatever the code currently does passes identically. MISSING LEVER: the same one named in T-repin-coding-prompt-sha256 — a proof form for 'a test re-pinned onto the intended behaviour after verifying that behaviour is the intended one'. The verification here (DS's branch-builder constraint is real; emit_merge_proof exists) lives in reading the code, not in an assertion.
